name: Smog_Mech

infobox.Systems: Smoldering Shells(Ranged)Effect ()Damage and apply Fire to a tile while adding Smoke to adjacent tiles.(1 damage)Upgrade IMore Smoke ()Adds Smoke to more tiles.Upgrade II+2 Damage ()Increases the center tile damage by 2.Nanofilter Mending(Passive)EffectStanding on Smoke repairs Mechs and removes Smoke.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theMist Eaterssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Smog Mech brings a mix of damage and utility with its Smoldering Shells weapon and Nanofilter Mending passive, providing a way to both neutralize several enemies while increasing the survivability of the Squad it is in. This combination of default equipment makes it one of the most versatile Mechs in the game.
Smoldering Shells can set one enemy onfirewhile cancelling attacks of surrounding Vek with smoke. It also deals respectable damage, starting with 1 point of direct damage + 1 more from fire, for 2 total starting damage. Though expensive in reactor cores, bothMore Smokeand+2 damageare solid upgrades, and good candidates for earlier cores; More Smoke is typically recommended first, as it lets it neutralize more threats significantly and heal more allies from more flexible positions. The +2 damage upgrade deals 3 damage directly, +1 more from fire damage, for a total of 4 damage, which is excellent for Ranged class Mechs. Spawns can also be ignited to preemptively deal damage to emerging Vek; do note however that Smoke will cancel out any fire tiles, and put out fire on enemies as well.
Nanofilter Mending negates the main downside of Smoke preventing Mechs using an action on it, allowing free movement on tiles with smoke while absorbing it for an action-free repair. This is a particularly useful passive when the More Smoke upgrade from Smoldering Shells is powered, as the Smoke can be useful for reviving downed mechs and keeping mechs with self-damaging weapons active.
The Smog Mech works very well in manyCustom Squadcombinations. Some notable examples include theRocket Mech, which synergizes very well withStorm Generatorby letting all smoke deal passive damage; theLeap Mech, which can target Smoke withHydraulic Legsto push and damage multiple enemies and heal itself, letting it raise its damage earlier without worrying for survival; and theIce Mech, which can be repaired by targeting it with Smoke to remove the self-inflicted Freeze status fromCryo-Launcher. But in general, almost any Mech appreciates the additional survival and Smoke utility offered by the Smog Mech.
Good starting pilots for the Smog Mech include:
  - Kai MillerandMorgan Lejeune; early-game common enemies are eliminated in one hit once fire damage is taken into account (2 direct damage + 1 fire damage). Once upgraded, the maximum damage with aBoostis 5 counting fire, which is an important breakpoint for being able to eliminate most alphas.
  - ArchimedesandChen Rongare other great pilot options, as they can move return to a better position in the board after firing near a corner.
